title: Quick check: How AI Overviews work

questions.prompt: What is the technical name for the pattern Google uses to ground AI Overviews in real, current web content?
questions.explanation: Google explicitly names the technique in the AI Optimization Guide: 'Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG): A technique (also known as grounding) used to improve the quality, accuracy, and freshness of AI responses by relying on our core Search ranking systems to retrieve relevant, up-to-date web pages from our Search index.'

questions.prompt: What does 'query fan-out' mean in the context of Google's AI search features?

choices.text: Splitting one user query into multiple search ads.
Generating several related queries from the user's original one, searching each, and synthesizing an answer from the combined results. Means topical depth matters more than exact-keyword matching.
Sharing the answer across multiple user accounts.
Fanning the result across regional servers for load balancing.

questions.explanation: From Google's docs: 'A set of concurrent, related queries generated by the model to request more information and fetch additional relevant search results to address the user's query.' Your page doesn't need to match the exact query — being a strong result on a related fan-out query is enough to get cited.
